sections.paragraph:
κυρία, ας, ἡ (fem. form of the subst. adj. κύριος [q.v. I]. Rare and late as a proper name: Preisigke, Namenbuch 188; HBeyer-HLietzmann, D. jüd. Katakombe der Villa Torlonia ’31, ins 41)

① a woman of special status, lady, mistress ἡ κ. τοῦ ἔργου the housekeeper prob. the one in charge of the household work assignments (cp. Tob. 2:12, w. ref. to ‘employers’) GJs 2:2. Used in addressing a definite person (Plut., Mor. 271d; Epict., Ench. 40; Cass. Dio 48, 44; POxy 112, 1; 3; 7; 744, 2=Ltzm., Griech. Papyri2 1910, 7; letter in Dssm., LO 160 [LAE2 193, n. 6]; κ. μου μήτηρ TestAbr A 3 p. 79, 25 [Stone p. 6].—LXX uses κ. to designate the mistress as opposed to the slave; so also JosAs; Philo, Congr. Erud. Gr. 154; Jos., Ant. 17, 137; 139; PTebt 413, 1; 6; 20) Hv 1, 1, 5; 1, 2, 2; 1, 3, 3; 1, 4, 2; 2, 1, 3; 3, 1, 3; 8; 3, 2, 4; 3, 3, 1; 4f; 3, 4, 1; 3; 3, 5, 3; 3, 6, 5f; 3, 8, 2; 5f; 4, 2, 2f; 4, 3, 1. Many take 2J 1; 5 in this lit. sense (e.g. BWestcott, The Epistles of St. John 1886, 214; HPoggel, D. 2. u. 3. Brief d. Apostels Joh. 1896, 127ff). For the less formal sense dear found in pap s. M-M. s.v.—Other scholars consider it more probable that

② κ. is fig. here and refers to Christians as a group: congregation (s. ἀδελφή 2b), and ἐκλεκτὴ κυρία is usually translated the chosen (or elect) lady (so BWeiss et al.; s. BBresky, Das Verhältnis des zweiten Johannesbriefs zum dritten 1906, 2ff; FDölger, Ac V/3, ’36, 211–17, Domina mater ecclesia u. d. ‘Herrin’ im 2 J; ABrooke, ICC; RBultmann, Hermeneia comm. On the precarious nature of ref. to Lex. Cantabr. [p. 79f, w. note p. 119f, Houtsma; also Demetr. of Phaleron: 228 Fgm. 4 p. 961] s. H-JKlauck, ZNW 81, ’90, 135–42). For the imagery of God’s people as a woman s. Jer 4:31; 38:21; Zeph 3:14; Gal 4:25f; 1 Pt 5:13. The proposal (HGibbins, Exp. 6th ser., 6, 1902, 228f) to make ἐκλεκτή a proper noun and κυρία an adj. has little to recommend it.—DELG s.v. κύριος. M-M. TW.

fem. fem. = feminine

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

adj. adj. = adjective

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

Preisigke, Namenbuch Preisigke, Namenbuch = Namenbuch 1922

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

prob. prob. = probable, probably

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ref. ref. = reference(s)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Ltzm. Ltzm. = HLietzmann, commentator and editor of various works

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

LO LO = Licht vom Osten—s. Dssm., LO in List 6

LAE LAE , s. Dssm. LO—List 6

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Hv Hv = Visions

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Joh. Joh. = Johannine

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ICC ICC = International Critical Commentary—List 6

Hermeneia Hermeneia = commentary series—List 6

comm. comm. = commentary, -aries, commentator(s)

Lex. Lex. Cantabr. = Lexicon Rhetoricum Cantabrigiense—List 5

Demetr Demetr , III b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

H H = Hermas; cited, unless otherwise noted, according to MWhittaker; II a.d.—List 1

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

Exp. Exp. = Expositor—List 6

ser. ser. = series

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 576
